channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: CFL secures six-year broadcast partnership with Bell Media, DAZN and YouTube
item.description: The Canadian Football League (CFL) has announced a major six-year extension of its broadcast and media rights agreement, strengthening its partnerships with Bell Media, DAZN, and YouTube. The deal ens...

item.title: Tanzania names four golfers for Africa Region IV Championship in Mauritius
item.description: Tanzania is preparing to send a team of four golfers to represent the country at the Africa Region IV Men Team Championship, which will take place from June 2 to June 6 at the Tamarina Golf Club in Ma...

item.title: Analysis argues Trump remains deeply involved in shaping Republican midterm strategy despite claims of disinterest
item.description: In this opinion piece, media analyst Howard Kurtz challenges the claim that President Donald Trump is indifferent to the upcoming midterm elections. The article argues that Trump’s statement that he “...

item.title: Rising gun violence in Khayelitsha raises concern amid broader incidents across South Africa
item.description: Residents of Khayelitsha in Cape Town are reportedly living in fear following a recent spike in gun violence, with community crime monitors warning that the situation has become increasingly severe. A...

item.title: Key facts and first impressions of the new Toyota Land Cruiser FJ launched in South Africa
item.description: The new Toyota Land Cruiser FJ has officially been introduced to the South African motoring press in Cape Town, drawing significant attention as a compact yet rugged addition to the Land Cruiser famil...

item.title: DroneShield shareholders oppose remuneration report as Hamish McLennan appointed chairman
item.description: DroneShield shareholders have delivered a significant rebuke to the company’s leadership at its annual general meeting, voting against the remuneration report in what is known in Australia as a 'first...

item.title: The Globe and Mail Releases Canadian Bestseller Lists for May 30, 2026
item.description: The Globe and Mail published its national bestseller rankings for the week of May 30, 2026, using data compiled by BookNet Canada’s BNC SalesData service. The lists highlight the most popular books ac...

item.title: EU fines Temu €200 million for failing to prevent sale of illegal products, further penalties pending
item.description: Chinese online retailer Temu has been slapped with a €200 million fine by the European Union for failing to adequately prevent the sale of illegal products on its platform. The penalty comes after the...

item.title: High Court Temporarily Stops Plans for Ebola Quarantine Facilities Involving US Government in Kenya
item.description: The High Court of Kenya has issued urgent conservatory orders stopping the government and other parties from setting up any Ebola quarantine, isolation or treatment facilities linked to the United Sta...

item.title: CMA upgrades three firms to investment bank licences in Kenya
item.description: The Capital Markets Authority (CMA) of Kenya has approved the upgrade of three financial firms to full investment banking status as part of its ongoing efforts to deepen and strengthen the country’s c...

item.title: Austrian sentenced for planning foiled terror attack on Taylor Swift concert
item.description: An Austrian court has handed a 21-year-old a 15-year jail sentence after he admitted to planning a jihadist attack on a Taylor Swift concert in Vienna. The plot, which led to the cancellation of three...

item.title: SDP presidential candidate Adebayo outlines reform agenda and calls for democratic renewal in Nigeria
item.description: The presidential candidate of the Social Democratic Party (SDP), Adewole Adebayo, has formally unveiled his party’s vision for Nigeria, calling for what he described as a national rebirth ahead of the...

item.title: Overview of SheMeansBusiness May 2026 Edition
item.description: This edition of SheMeansBusiness, May 2026, showcases content focused on business updates and entrepreneurial insights. The e-edition provides readers with access to BusinessDay’s latest reports, indu...

item.title: Video game composers discuss AI concerns, indie innovation and the growing popularity of game soundtracks
item.description: The article explores the current state and future direction of video game music in 2026 through interviews with several prominent composers, including Austin Wintory, Lena Raine, Jesper Kyd, Gordy Haa...

item.title: Trinamool Congress faces internal disputes, resignations and public backlash in West Bengal after electoral setback
item.description: Three weeks after its electoral defeat in West Bengal, the Trinamool Congress (TMC) is witnessing multiple internal and external challenges, including infighting among senior leaders, resignations, co...

item.title: High Court Grants Temporary Protection to CHADEMA Against Registrar Actions
item.description: In Manyara, Tanzania, the High Court granted an interim injunction to CHADEMA, stopping the Registrar of Political Parties from taking any action against the party until the full case is heard. Justic...

item.title: Ishaq Dar holds Washington visit for talks with Marco Rubio amid regional tensions and Iran ceasefire discussions
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar reached Washington after completing a three-day visit to New York linked to United Nations engagements. According to Pakistan’s Foreign Office, Dar...

item.title: Helen Mirren expresses support for Tom Hardy amid MobLand production disputes
item.description: Dame Helen Mirren has publicly shown her support for Tom Hardy following reports that the actor had been removed from the British crime drama MobLand. Initial news suggested that Hardy, 48, had clashe...

item.title: Swellendam Police Station Commander Suspended Pending Investigation into Alleged Taxi Business Links
item.description: In a recent development that has raised concerns about police integrity in the Western Cape, the commander of the Swellendam Police Station has been suspended internally this month due to alleged ties...

item.title: Collapse of local industries leaves Musoma youth with limited livelihood options
item.description: In Musoma, the capital of Tanzania’s Mara region, many young people are facing a difficult economic reality as once-thriving industries continue to shut down. The article highlights how the collapse o...
